metadata.title: The Ultraviolet Catastrophe
metadata.notes: Fresh build. GRAPHIC ~53% of beats accepted deliberately (chart arc = evidence section; MOTION.md short-form corollary).
metadata.description_blurb: By 1900, the glow of hot objects had been measured to within one percent — and the best theory in physics predicted it infinitely wrong. The story of the ultraviolet catastrophe, and the desperate curve-fit that started quantum mechanics.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: NO RENDERABLE BEATS: no beat carries a shot.remotion.pattern or Manim annotation — the pipeline will render NOTHING and every slot becomes a YOU-slate. The sheet was authored without the scene contract.
B01: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B16: palette=claude but the outro is 'un-annotated' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. Heat a bar of iron and it glows. Red, then orange, then yellow-white. Any iron, any ceramic, anything at that temperature — the same color, the same glow.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Heat a bar of iron and it glows. Red, then orange, then yellow-white. Any iron, any ceramic, anything at that temperature — the same color, the same glow.

shot.note: Higgsfield i2v motion from the archival still (seed credited in sidecar) — synthetic motion, disclose in credits

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] foundry interior, worker with glowing iron bar, early 1900s; set shot.focus on the glow once the image lands

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [PHOTO] foundry interior, worker with glowing iron bar, early 1900s; set shot.focus on the glow once the image lands

beats.narration_text: By 1900, that glow had been measured to within one percent. The best theory in physics predicted it infinitely wrong. Literally infinitely.
beats.tts_normalized_text: By 1900, that glow had been measured to within one percent. The best theory in physics predicted it infinitely wrong. Literally infinitely.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: By 1900, that glow had been measured to within one percent. The best theory in physics predicted it infinitely wrong. Literally infinitely.

beats.narration_text: In Berlin, Otto Lummer and Ernst Pringsheim traced the spectrum of heated bodies with exquisite care. Smooth, hump-shaped curves, reproducible to a percent. The data was solid.
beats.tts_normalized_text: In Berlin, Otto Lummer and Ernst Pringsheim traced the spectrum of heated bodies with exquisite care. Smooth, hump-shaped curves, reproducible to a percent. The data was solid.

shot.note: Higgsfield i2v motion from the archival still (seed credited in sidecar) — synthetic motion, disclose in credits

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] Physikalisch-Technische Reichsanstalt laboratory / Lummer or Pringsheim at spectroscopy bench, circa 1900

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [PHOTO] Physikalisch-Technische Reichsanstalt laboratory / Lummer or Pringsheim at spectroscopy bench, circa 1900

beats.narration_text: Every temperature drew the same shape: a hump. Hotter meant taller, with the peak sliding toward blue. Explaining this hump was the whole game.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Every temperature drew the same shape: a hump. Hotter meant taller, with the peak sliding toward blue. Explaining this hump was the whole game.

viz.data: axes: frequency vs spectral energy density; navy measured hump, draw-on; second fainter hump at higher T, peak shifted right
viz.note: VERIFY hump shapes against Planck curve at T=3000K and T=4500K

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Every temperature drew the same shape: a hump. Hotter meant taller, with the peak sliding toward blue. Explaining this hump was the whole game.

beats.narration_text: The classical argument was clean. Take a glowing cavity. Count the ways light can vibrate inside it. That count is pure geometry — nobody disputed it.
beats.tts_normalized_text: The classical argument was clean. Take a glowing cavity. Count the ways light can vibrate inside it. That count is pure geometry — nobody disputed it.

shot.note: Higgsfield i2v motion from the archival still (seed credited in sidecar) — synthetic motion, disclose in credits

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO+ANNOTATION] kiln or furnace interior plate; annotation plane draws 3-4 standing-wave arcs inside the cavity mouth (terracotta, hand-drawn) — annotation lands at assembly, plate slots now

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [PHOTO+ANNOTATION] kiln or furnace interior plate; annotation plane draws 3-4 standing-wave arcs inside the cavity mouth (terracotta, hand-drawn) — annotation lands at assembly, plate slots now

beats.narration_text: Then classical physics pays every vibration the same wage: k T of energy, each. Equal shares. The same rule that ran the steam age flawlessly.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Then classical physics pays every vibration the same wage: kay tee of energy, each. Equal shares. The same rule that ran the steam age flawlessly.

viz.data: isotype square grid, ~30 modes, each square receives an identical 'kT' chip; count-up in reading order, lag_ratio from audio window
viz.note: equipartition: 2 quadratic terms per EM mode -> kBT per mode (chapter)

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Then classical physics pays every vibration the same wage: k T of energy, each. Equal shares. The same rule that ran the steam age flawlessly.

beats.narration_text: Modes times wages gives the Rayleigh–Jeans law. At long wavelengths it fits the Berlin data. Then the mode count keeps climbing — and the prediction runs away.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Modes times wages gives the Rayleigh Jeans law. At long wavelengths it fits the Berlin data. Then the mode count keeps climbing — and the prediction runs away.

viz.data: same axes as V04; crimson RJ curve draws left-to-right, hugs the navy data at low frequency, then diverges upward off-frame; runaway timed to 'runs away'
viz.note: RJ: u = (8 pi nu^2 / c^3) kBT

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Modes times wages gives the Rayleigh–Jeans law. At long wavelengths it fits the Berlin data. Then the mode count keeps climbing — and the prediction runs away.

beats.narration_text: Add every frequency and the total is infinite. Taken literally, a hot poker should flood your living room with ultraviolet. It plainly does not.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Add every frequency and the total is infinite. Taken literally, a hot poker should flood your living room with ultraviolet. It plainly does not.

shot.note: Higgsfield i2v from the stoker still — synthetic motion, disclose in credits

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] domestic hearth with fire iron / poker, early 1900s interior; set shot.focus on the poker

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [PHOTO] domestic hearth with fire iron / poker, early 1900s interior; set shot.focus on the poker

beats.narration_text: So which assumption breaks? Not the counting. Not equal shares — gases obey it beautifully. The killer is subtler: that energy flows continuously, in any amount whatsoever.
beats.tts_normalized_text: So which assumption breaks? Not the counting. Not equal shares — gases obey it beautifully. The killer is subtler: that energy flows continuously, in any amount whatsoever.

viz.data: three serif labels stacked: 'counting the modes' (check), 'equal shares' (check), 'energy flows continuously' — third gets the terracotta hand-drawn ring on the narration cue
viz.note: one editor's-pen voice: the ring only

build.suggested: suggested prompt: So which assumption breaks? Not the counting. Not equal shares — gases obey it beautifully. The killer is subtler: that energy flows continuously, in any amount

beats.narration_text: Max Planck was not trying to start a revolution. He was trying to fit a curve — working backward from the data, and honest about it.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Max Planck was not trying to start a revolution. He was trying to fit a curve — working backward from the data, and honest about it.

shot.note: Higgsfield i2v from archival Planck still — disclosure in credits; seed in sidecar

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] Max Planck at desk or blackboard, circa 1900; set shot.focus on his face

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [PHOTO] Max Planck at desk or blackboard, circa 1900; set shot.focus on his face

beats.narration_text: The only fix that worked: the cavity's oscillators cannot hold just any energy. Only whole steps. Zero, one, two, three chunks — nothing in between.
beats.tts_normalized_text: The only fix that worked: the cavity's oscillators cannot hold just any energy. Only whole steps. Zero, one, two, three chunks — nothing in between.

viz.data: energy staircase: levels 0, hv, 2hv, 3hv as slate steps; a small marker tries to sit between steps and snaps to the nearest one on the cue 'nothing in between'
viz.note: Planck quantized WALL OSCILLATORS, not the field (chapter) — keep narration faithful to that

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The only fix that worked: the cavity's oscillators cannot hold just any energy. Only whole steps. Zero, one, two, three chunks — nothing in between.

beats.narration_text: One line carries the revolution: E equals h nu. A chunk's energy is the light's frequency times a new constant of nature. That equals sign is a ban.
beats.tts_normalized_text: One line carries the revolution: E equals h new. A chunk's energy is the light's frequency times a new constant of nature. That equals sign is a ban.

tangent.lhs: The energy of one chunk of light.
tangent.rhs: Its frequency, times a new constant of nature.
tangent.claim: Exactly equal — in-between energies are banned.

example.scenario: A forge at 3000 K. Thermal budget per mode: 0.26 eV.
example.cost: High frequencies go dark. The catastrophe is cancelled.

tangent.reentry: And that is the whole fix. Back to the curve.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: One line carries the revolution: E equals h nu. A chunk's energy is the light's frequency times a new constant of nature. That equals sign is a ban.

beats.narration_text: E, the energy of one chunk. Nu, the light's frequency. And h — Planck's constant — the exchange rate between them. Ten to the minus thirty-four. Tiny.
beats.tts_normalized_text: E, the energy of one chunk. New, the light's frequency. And h — Planck's constant — the exchange rate between them. Ten to the minus thirty-four. Tiny.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: E, the energy of one chunk. Nu, the light's frequency. And h — Planck's constant — the exchange rate between them. Ten to the minus thirty-four. Tiny.

beats.narration_text: At a forge's temperature, red chunks cost almost nothing. Ultraviolet chunks cost fifty times the thermal budget. The cavity cannot pay. High frequencies stay dark.
beats.tts_normalized_text: At a forge's temperature, red chunks cost almost nothing. Ultraviolet chunks cost fifty times the thermal budget. The cavity cannot pay. High frequencies stay dark.

viz.note: VERIFY: chapter worked example is x=47.9 at nu=3e15 Hz, T=3000K; red 1.8 eV is ~660nm — narration says 'fifty times', example card says 48x

build.suggested: suggested prompt: At a forge's temperature, red chunks cost almost nothing. Ultraviolet chunks cost fifty times the thermal budget. The cavity cannot pay. High frequencies stay d

beats.narration_text: Planck's formula traced the measurements within one percent. Where classical physics worked, it agrees exactly. Where classical physics exploded, it dies away exponentially. No catastrophe.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Planck's formula traced the measurements within one percent. Where classical physics worked, it agrees exactly. Where classical physics exploded, it dies away exponentially. No catastrophe.

viz.data: return to V04/V07 axes: crimson runaway greys out; navy Planck curve draws through the measured data points; label 'PLANCK 1900' chip
viz.note: low-freq limit -> kBT (matches RJ); high-freq -> exponential falloff (chapter)

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Planck's formula traced the measurements within one percent. Where classical physics worked, it agrees exactly. Where classical physics exploded, it dies away e

beats.narration_text: In the ultraviolet, the classical prediction is off by twenty orders of magnitude. Not wrong by a little. Wrong by a one with twenty zeros.
beats.tts_normalized_text: In the ultraviolet, the classical prediction is off by twenty orders of magnitude. Not wrong by a little. Wrong by a one with twenty zeros.

viz.data: number beat: '10²⁰' large serif; beneath, small mono '100,000,000,000,000,000,000 ×'; zeros count up on the cue 'twenty zeros'
viz.note: VERIFY: ratio 7e-20 at x=47.9 (chapter worked example)

build.suggested: suggested prompt: In the ultraviolet, the classical prediction is off by twenty orders of magnitude. Not wrong by a little. Wrong by a one with twenty zeros.

beats.narration_text: He read it to the Physical Society on December 14th, 1900 — his constant within one percent of today's value. It reads like an apology, not a manifesto.
beats.tts_normalized_text: He read it to the Physical Society on December fourteenth, nineteen hundred — his constant within one percent of today's value. It reads like an apology, not a manifesto.
beats.new_visual_element: [SCAN] Annalen der Physik 309, 553 (1901), 'Über das Gesetz der Energieverteilung im Normalspectrum' — title page; highlighter sweep on title, zoom-to-passage at assembly; renders as hold+slow-zoom until Remotion plane lands

build.needs: YOU → drop a historical image at media/B14.png (or .mp4)

beats.narration_text: Planck spent years trying to remove the chunks from his own formula. It cannot be done. Who was this reluctant revolutionary? That's a story of its own.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Planck spent years trying to remove the chunks from his own formula. It cannot be done. Who was this reluctant revolutionary? That's a story of its own.

shot.note: Higgsfield i2v from archival Planck still — disclosure in credits; seed in sidecar

beats.narration_text: Next: light itself comes apart in chunks — the photoelectric effect.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Next: light itself comes apart in chunks — the photoelectric effect.
